mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference between falling and rising FEDFUNDS periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, it signals labor market normalization that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 8.0 in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized expansion in construction and broader employment, which amplifies aggregate demand and lifts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the combined series do not predict returns better than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 95.0, it reflects near-full capacity utilization that typically precedes supply constraints and margin compression, causing S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO stays above 95.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.5 percent signals robust economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant difference between quarters with GDP growth above versus below the 2.5 percent trend.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling unemployment indicates strengthening consumer confidence and purchasing power, which synergistically boosts corporate revenue forecasts and lifts equity prices over a two-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative predictive relationship for 60-day returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample periods, indicating the interaction does not capture forward momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth by more than 2 percent, it signals a supply glut that erodes pricing power and profit margins, causing S&P 500 returns to decline over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth by > 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where the INDPRO-GDPC1 growth differential exceeds 2.0 percent.
